title: pteris inaequalis var. aequata
intro: Pteris inaequalis var. aequata, also known as the Ferny-leaved Brake, is a type of fern plant belonging to the Pteris genus, which is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Pteris inaequalis var. aequata include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, and are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
